content_en: He was one of the greatest scholars in hadith and a companion of Ahmad ibn Hanbal. Zaid ibn Thaabit [w,>t:i 0! .l:lj]: Zaid ibn Thaabit (d. 45/665) was born in Madinah some eleven years before the event of the Prophet's hijrah. However, he was raised in Makkah. He was one of the Companions of the Prophet (peace be upon him) who was assigned the job of recording the revelations of the Quran. Later, he would play an important role during the time of Abu Bakr and during the time of Uthmaan in compiling the complete and authentic Quran. He was greatly respected by the other Companions, in particular, Umar would leave him in charge ofMadinah whenever he would have to leave the city. Al-Zaila'ee [Íjll]: Jamaal al-Deen Abdullah ibn Yoosuf al-Zayla'ee (d. 762/1 360) was a Hanafi jurist, legal theorist and scholar of hadith. He was born in Zayla' in Somalia and died in Cairo. His Nasb al-Raayah fl Takhreej Ahaadeeth al­ Hidaayah is a work on the hadith referred to in the famous work of Hanafi fiqh, al-Hidaayah. Al-Zarkashi [׋.Jjll]: Badr al-Deen Muhammad al-Zarkashi (745/1344-794/1 392) was of Turkish background but born in Egypt. He became a leading Shafi'ee scholar as well as a scholar of hadith and Quranic sciences. His work on Quranic sciences, al-Burhaan fl Uloom al-Quran, has since been a standard. He has an important work on Shafi'ee legal theory entitled, al-Bahir al­ Muheet. He also wrote an important treatise on legal maxims from a Shafi'ee perspective, al-Manthoor fl al-Qawaaid. Al-Zuhri, Muhammad ibn Shihaab [lf.J4jll YÊ 0! ª]: Muhammad ibn Muslim ibn Abdullah al-Zuhri (58/678-124/742) was born in Madinah. He was instrumental in developing some of the aspects of the sciences of hadith. He became insistent on the use of the isnaad and he began to record hadith in a systematic fashion. He was also a very influential jurist and one of the leaders of the Madinan school of fiqh. His thought greatly influenced Imam Malik. He spent part of his life in Syria and died close to the Hijaz-Palestine border. Glossary Glossary Abu (Y.I) - "father of'; in Arabic, many times the proper form is Abi (as in ibn Abi Shaibah), however since that form is unfamiliar to many readers, Abu is used throughout this work. Adhaan (ul.:il)- the call to prayer. Adi ( Jlc) -justice, righteousness Ahl al-Hill wa al-Aqd ( .ii.JIJ J:,...l JA I) - the most respected and influential people in Islamic society; they have the most important role in determining who is to be the ruler and when should the ruler be removed. Ahl al-ra'i (':?1)1 JAi)- this is a term used for those scholars who were more apt to make ijtihaad or personal reasoning; many times it is used to refer to the Iraqi/Kuran jurists as a whole. Ahl al-Suffah (u....!I JAi) - the poor people who used to live in the Prophet's mosque Ahl al-sunnah wa al-Jamaah ('-c\...;JIJ I JAi) - "The People of the Sunnah and the Congregation," this refers to those people who follow the way of the sunnah of the Prophet (peace be upon him) and the way of his Companions with respect to beliefs and deeds or a general application of the religion of Islam. Alaqah (ഖ) - "something that clings and adheres," one of the stages that a human goes through in the fetus Allaahu akbar (fa.I ..ii ) - "Allah is greater (than anything)" Amal ( J=) - deed, act Ameer (->-""I) - leader; often used for the head or president of a mosque An (UC-) - "on the authority of' Ansaar (J ... .:>.il) - the Muslim residents ofMadinah who welcomed their brothers who had emigrated from Makkah to Madinah Ansaari ((,?_J ....... .il) - a person from the Ansaar Aqeeqah (ഗ) - the sunnah of slaughtering an animal due to the birth of a child Arafah (Ǳ y:.) - This is a place that a person is required to visit as one of the rites of the pilgrimage; it is visited on the 9th day of the month of the pilgrimage. Arkaan (ulS))- these are pillars or n׌cessary aspects of a particular deed. Asbaab al-nuwol (J.Jjll '·:·A...il) - circumstances surrounding the revelation of particular verses of the Quran Asbaab wurood al-hadith ( 6;.i:...1 ୈJ.JJ y4'°-I) - circumstances surrounding particular speech of the Prophet (peace be upon him). Ashaairah, al- (oy:.L!.':/I) - These are the supposed followers of Abu al-Hasan al-Ashari (260-324 A.H.). This group is known for making taweel for many of the attributes of Allah. They also believe that imaan is solely tasdeeq. Ashaab al-ra'i (':?1.)1 yb.....l)- this is a term used for those scholars who were more apt to make ijtihaad or personal reasoning; in reality, though, it was used as a general reference for the jurists ofKufah and Iraq or those of the Hanafi school.
title_id: Data Biografis Para Ulama (lanjutan) dan Glosarium
content_id:
Yahya ibn Main (lanjutan): Beliau adalah salah satu ulama terbesar dalam ilmu hadis dan sahabat Ahmad ibn Hanbal yang setia.

Zaid ibn Tsabit (w. 45/665 H): Beliau lahir di Madinah sekitar sebelas tahun sebelum peristiwa Hijrah Nabi ﷺ, namun dibesarkan di Makkah. Beliau adalah salah satu Sahabat Nabi ﷺ yang ditugaskan untuk mencatat wahyu Al-Quran. Kemudian, beliau memainkan peran penting pada masa Abu Bakr dan Utsman dalam mengompilasi Al-Quran secara lengkap dan otentik. Beliau sangat dihormati oleh para Sahabat lainnya; khususnya Umar senantiasa meninggalkan beliau sebagai penjabat di Madinah setiap kali beliau harus meninggalkan kota.

Jamal al-Din Abdullah ibn Yusuf al-Zayla'i (w. 762/1360 H): Beliau adalah ahli hukum Hanafi, teoris hukum, dan ulama hadis. Beliau lahir di Zayla' di Somalia dan wafat di Kairo. Karyanya Nasb al-Rayah fi Takhrij Ahadits al-Hidayah membahas hadis-hadis yang dirujuk dalam karya fiqih Hanafi terkenal, al-Hidayah.

Badr al-Din Muhammad al-Zarkasyi (745/1344-794/1392 H): Beliau berlatarbelakang Turki namun lahir di Mesir. Beliau menjadi ulama Syafi'i terkemuka sekaligus ulama hadis dan ilmu-ilmu Al-Quran. Karyanya tentang ilmu-ilmu Al-Quran, al-Burhan fi Ulum al-Quran, sejak saat itu menjadi standar rujukan. Beliau memiliki karya penting tentang teori hukum Syafi'i berjudul al-Bahr al-Muhit, serta risalah penting tentang kaidah-kaidah hukum dari perspektif Syafi'i, al-Mansur fi al-Qawa'id.

Muhammad ibn Muslim ibn Abdullah al-Zuhri (58/678-124/742 H): Beliau lahir di Madinah. Beliau berperan penting dalam mengembangkan beberapa aspek ilmu hadis. Beliau bersikeras menggunakan isnad dan mulai mencatat hadis secara sistematis. Beliau juga seorang fakih yang sangat berpengaruh dan salah satu pemimpin mazhab fiqih Madinah. Pemikirannya sangat mempengaruhi Imam Malik. Beliau menghabiskan sebagian hidupnya di Suriah dan wafat di dekat perbatasan Hijaz-Palestina.

Glosarium: Abu — 'ayah dari'; dalam bahasa Arab, bentuk yang benar sering kali adalah Abi (seperti dalam Ibn Abi Syaibah), namun karena bentuk tersebut tidak familiar bagi banyak pembaca, maka Abu digunakan di seluruh karya ini. Adzan — seruan untuk salat. Adl — keadilan, kebenaran. Ahl al-Hall wal-'Aqd — orang-orang yang paling dihormati dan berpengaruh dalam masyarakat Islam; mereka memiliki peran terpenting dalam menentukan siapa yang akan menjadi penguasa dan kapan penguasa harus dicopot. Ahl al-Ra'y — istilah yang digunakan untuk para ulama yang lebih cenderung melakukan ijtihad atau penalaran pribadi; sering digunakan untuk merujuk pada para ahli hukum Iraq/Kufah secara keseluruhan. Ahl al-Suffah — kaum miskin yang biasa tinggal di masjid Nabi ﷺ. Ahl al-Sunnah wal-Jama'ah — 'Golongan Sunnah dan Jamaah', merujuk pada orang-orang yang mengikuti jalan sunnah Nabi ﷺ dan jalan para Sahabatnya dalam hal akidah, amal perbuatan, dan penerapan agama Islam secara umum. Alaqah — 'sesuatu yang menempel dan melekat', salah satu tahap yang dilalui manusia dalam masa janin. Allahu Akbar — 'Allah Mahabesar (dari segalanya)'. Amal — perbuatan, tindakan. Amir — pemimpin; sering digunakan untuk kepala atau presiden sebuah masjid. 'An — 'atas otoritas dari'. Anshar — penduduk Muslim Madinah yang menyambut saudara-saudara mereka yang berhijrah dari Makkah ke Madinah. Anshari — seseorang dari kalangan Anshar. Aqiqah — sunah menyembelih hewan karena kelahiran seorang anak. Arafah — tempat yang wajib dikunjungi seseorang sebagai salah satu ritual haji; dikunjungi pada hari ke-9 bulan Zulhijjah. Arkan — ini adalah pilar-pilar atau aspek-aspek yang diperlukan dari suatu ibadah tertentu. Asbab al-Nuzul — keadaan-keadaan yang melingkupi turunnya ayat-ayat tertentu dari Al-Quran. Asbab Wurud al-Hadits — keadaan-keadaan yang melingkupi sabda-sabda tertentu dari Nabi ﷺ. Al-Asy'ariyyah — para pengikut Abu al-Hasan al-Asy'ari (260-324 H). Kelompok ini dikenal karena melakukan ta'wil terhadap banyak sifat-sifat Allah. Mereka juga berpendapat bahwa iman hanya berupa tasdiq (pembenaran hati). Ashab al-Ra'y — istilah untuk para ulama yang lebih cenderung melakukan ijtihad atau penalaran pribadi; pada kenyataannya, istilah ini digunakan sebagai rujukan umum untuk para ahli hukum Kufah dan Iraq atau mereka yang bermazhab Hanafi.
content_ar: He was one of the greatest العلماء in الحديث and a companion of أحمد ابن حنبل. Zaid ibn Thaabit [w,>t:i 0! .l:lj]: Zaid ibn Thaabit (d. 45/665) was born in المدينة المنورة some eleven years قبل the event of النبي ﷺ's hijrah. غير أن, he was raised in مكة المكرمة. He was one of the الصحابة of النبي ﷺ (ﷺ) who was assigned the job of recording the revelations of the القرآن الكريم. Later, he would play an المهم role خلال the time of أبو بكر and خلال the time of Uthmaan in compiling the complete and authentic القرآن الكريم. He was greatly respected by the other الصحابة, وبالأخص, عمر would leave him in charge ofMadinah كلما he would have to leave the city. Al-Zaila'ee [Íjll]: Jamaal al-Deen Abdullah ibn Yoosuf al-Zayla'ee (d. 762/1 360) was a الحنفي jurist, legal theorist and scholar of الحديث. He was born in Zayla' in Somalia and died in Cairo. His Nasb al-Raayah fl Takhreej Ahaadeeth al­ Hidaayah is a work on the الحديث referred to in the famous work of الحنفي الفقه, al-Hidaayah. Al-Zarkashi [׋.Jjll]: Badr al-Deen محمد ﷺ al-Zarkashi (745/1344-794/1 392) was of Turkish background but born in Egypt. He became a leading Shafi'ee scholar as well as a scholar of الحديث and القرآني sciences. His work on القرآني sciences, al-Burhaan fl Uloom al-القرآن الكريم, has بما أن been a standard. He has an المهم work on Shafi'ee legal theory entitled, al-Bahir al­ Muheet. He أيضاً wrote an المهم treatise on legal maxims from a Shafi'ee perspective, al-Manthoor fl al-Qawaaid. Al-Zuhri, محمد ﷺ ibn Shihaab [lf.J4jll YÊ 0! ª]: محمد ﷺ ibn مسلم ibn Abdullah al-Zuhri (58/678-124/742) was born in المدينة المنورة. He was instrumental in developing some of the aspects of the sciences of الحديث. He became insistent on the use of the isnaad and he began to record الحديث in a systematic fashion. He was أيضاً a very influential jurist and one of the leaders of the Madinan school of الفقه. His thought greatly influenced الإمام مالك. He spent part of his الحياة in Syria and died close to the Hijaz-Palestine border. Ahl al-ra'i (':?1)1 JAi)- this is a term used for those العلماء who were more apt to make ijtihaad or personal reasoning; many times it is used to refer to the Iraqi/Kuran jurists as a whole.
